title: Monitoring Approaches Compared
subtitle: OmniDrone against the real alternatives — honest scoring
description: A capability comparison across the approaches a farm actually chooses between. The OmniDrone wins on depth, coverage, and imaging; it LOSES on continuity, weather tolerance, cost, and regulatory simplicity. An honest radar shows the losses. The takeaway is not that we dominate — it is that a fixed-buoy + OmniDrone hybrid dominates either alone, which is where the product roadmap points.
honest_note: Scores are the authors' qualitative judgement on a 1-5 scale, not measured benchmarks. A vendor-scored comparison always flatters the vendor; this one deliberately scores our own weaknesses low so the comparison is credible. See competitive-landscape.json.
the_honest_reading: OmniDrone and the buoy have nearly complementary shapes — we are strong exactly where the buoy is weak (depth, space, imaging) and weak where it is strong (continuity, weather, cost, regulation). That complementarity is the argument for a hybrid, not for displacement. The USV matches us on depth and beats us on weather and continuity; our answer to it is imaging and topology-independence. This chart should be shown with the conclusion stated, not left for the viewer to misread as domination.
